attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

codes_manifest.display: Urinary tract infection, site not specified
Ceftriaxone (candidate substance concept; AMT trade/product code pending terminology receipt)

transform_flags: Patient age not stated in source; inferred as ~82 (older_adult) from 'elderly', nursing-home residency and bedbound baseline — clinician to confirm on review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-ID-04-00046 — SEQ derived from source AUC-046; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-ID-04 bucket.

Specialty assigned ID (Infectious Disease) as primary with EMG and URO secondary (schema enum has no GERI/geriatrics code, so the older-adult framing is carried in age_band + contextual_modifiers instead) — reviewer may prefer EMG as primary; case_metadata is authoritative over the id per taxonomy id_rule.

difficulty_tier set to atypical_presentation_high_risk (elderly sepsis presenting as delirium/collapse, high mortality risk); reviewer may reclassify (communication_barrier and multi_morbidity_complex were also candidates).

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present nursing-home clinician who assessed perfusion, positioned the patient, administered IM ceftriaxone 1 g and dispatched an ICP ambulance. Reprojected to AI-appropriate recognise + escalate (000) + safe holding measures; IM/IV drugs and IV fluids are recorded as the emergency-pathway treatment delivered by paramedics/ED, never issued by the AI Doctor.

Objective split: nurse/caregiver-reported vitals and video-observable findings (temp, BP, HR, RR, SpO2, mottling, drowsiness, oliguria) placed in 01.objective_data_offered as caregiver_reported/video_observable with verified:false and reliability caveats; clinician-only findings (GCS 13, capillary refill 4s, chest auscultation, serum lactate, blood cultures) sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits.

AMT code for ceftriaxone recorded as a candidate SNOMED substance concept (373476007), not a verified AMT trade-product code — flagged for terminology-receipt resolution; dose 1 g IM/IV taken verbatim from the source note (adult, not paediatric), no dose synthesised.

Serum lactate and blood-culture results are described in the source as 'expected' findings; captured as sealed investigation_result symptoms (present_in_case:true) representing the correct hospital workup, not values obtainable via telehealth.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
